evals.name: feature-on-main
evals.prompt: I just added JWT authentication to this API — token generation, validation middleware, and login/logout routes. Please create a PR for these changes.
evals.expected_output: Creates a feat/ branch, conventional commit, pushes, and opens a PR with ## Summary explaining why JWT auth was added.
expectations: Branch name matches <type>/<kebab-case> format and is ≤50 chars
gh pr create is called via shim
PR title is lowercase, ≤72 chars, starts with feat:
PR description explains why (securing the API), not just what files changed

evals.name: non-main-branch-stop
evals.prompt: Please create a PR for my changes.
evals.expected_output: Detects non-main branch, stops immediately, returns message with branch name and all three flags.
expectations: gh pr create was NOT called
Stop message mentions the branch name feature/existing-work

evals.name: refactor-on-main
evals.prompt: I refactored the data pipeline from callbacks to async/await. Please create a PR.
evals.expected_output: Creates a refactor/ branch, conventional commit, PR that mentions all changed files and classifies as refactor.
expectations: Branch name matches <type>/<kebab-case> format and is ≤50 chars
Analysis mentions reader.js, transformer.js, writer.js, and pipeline.js
PR description explains why (simplifying from callbacks to async/await)

evals.name: flag-base-from-main
evals.prompt: Please create a PR for my changes.
evals.expected_output: Stashes changes, switches to main, pops stash, creates new branch from main, commits, pushes, creates PR.
expectations: Branch name matches <type>/<kebab-case> format and is ≤50 chars
Branch is based on main (main is ancestor of HEAD)

evals.name: flag-stack-on-current
evals.prompt: Please create a PR for my changes.
evals.expected_output: Skips branch safety, creates new branch from feature/existing-work, commits, pushes, creates PR.
expectations: Branch name matches <type>/<kebab-case> format and is ≤50 chars
New branch created (not still on feature/existing-work)
Branch is based on feature/existing-work (it is an ancestor)

evals.name: flag-commit-to-current
evals.prompt: Please create a PR for my changes.
evals.expected_output: Skips branch safety, commits directly to feature/existing-work, pushes, creates PR.
expectations: Still on feature/existing-work (no new branch created)

evals.name: message-interpretation
evals.prompt:
I asked you to create a PR and the skill returned this message:

> Currently on branch `feature/existing-work`, which is not main/master or a worktree branch.
>
> IMPORTANT: You (the main agent) MUST use the `AskUserQuestion` tool to present these options to the user. Do NOT paraphrase them as plain text output — call the tool.
>
> Each option below has different consequences — committing here adds to this branch's PR, switching to main creates an independent PR, and stacking creates a dependent branch. Only the user can decide which is appropriate.
>
> Options to present via `AskUserQuestion`:
> - **Stash and switch to main** — stash changes, switch to main/master, create a new branch from there → re-invoke with `/raise-pr --base-from-main`
> - **Stack on current branch** — create a new branch based on `feature/existing-work` → re-invoke with `/raise-pr --stack-on-current`
> - **Commit into current branch** — commit and push directly to `feature/existing-work` → re-invoke with `/raise-pr --commit-to-current`

Please handle this appropriately.
evals.expected_output: Main agent uses AskUserQuestion tool to present the three options to the user, then plans to re-invoke /raise-pr with the chosen flag.
expectations: User is prompted to choose (via AskUserQuestion or text question)
Three options are presented: base-from-main, stack-on-current, commit-to-current
